runs.content:
**Investigation idea:** "Me, Myself, and AI"'s new BofA reskilling episode is a ready primary source for ai-future-of-work.md's own unresolved retraining question. podcasts/suggestions.md's newest batch (week of 2026-07-23) recommends Me, Myself, and AI specifically for its Season 13 finale — Bank of America's own account of reskilling a global workforce for AI — as a direct match for the "AI impact on work" thread in Fred's profile, from the operator side rather than founder/researcher side. ai-tech/ai-future-of-work.md already has an explicit unresolved Open Question: "Is there a policy response that actually works? ... Retraining programmes historically have poor track records" — but the page cites no primary account of an employer's retraining programme actually being tried. It also sits directly adjacent to work/source-workday-disruption-debate-2026.md's already-flagged "compliance is the underappreciated bloat" theme — a large regulated bank is exactly the kind of enterprise where that tension would show up first. Next step: once Fred listens to the BofA episode, add its specific findings to ai-future-of-work.md's Open Questions section.

**Cross-domain connection:** A brand-new podcast recommendation frames crypto exactly the way Finance's own macro-investing stub needs it framed — and Crypto's own overview page doesn't have the concept at all. podcasts/suggestions.md's newest batch recommends On The Margin, explicitly because it treats institutional crypto allocation "as a macro instrument," with its flagship sample episode framing BTC as a hedge against sovereign-debt dynamics. finance/concepts/concept-global-macro.md is still a stub built entirely from equities/rates/collateral material — zero mention of crypto or Bitcoin, despite Fred holding BTC/ETH/LINK. Crypto - overview.md's own "Key Concepts to Build Out" checklist is exclusively DeFi-mechanics — no macro-framing item either, even though BTC/ETH are Fred's most conventional holdings. Different angle from the already-logged 2026-07-05 AI-capex-bubble/crypto connection — that one was about small AI-narrative tokens; this is the mainstream "BTC as macro hedge" thesis missing entirely. Next step: seed a "Crypto as macro asset" section in concept-global-macro.md, cross-linked from Crypto - overview, once Fred picks up the show.

Thursday focus (Books + Podcasts): Books domain quiet this run — no new books added since Gladiator (still missing its own summary page, already an open item) and no change to the July suggestions batch. Podcasts had four fresh recommendations today; the two above were the ones that landed on genuine existing gaps rather than just extending an already-covered niche.

**Cross-domain connection:** New York's first-in-nation data-centre moratorium is a new, unaddressed demand-side risk to Finance's two AI-power picks-and-shovels ideas. ai-tech/source-trump-ai-executive-order-gold-eagle-2026-07-19.md documents Gov. Hochul's executive order pausing state environmental permits for up to a year for any data centre consuming ≥50MW — the first statewide U.S. moratorium, with the state legislature separately advancing a broader bill and 46% voter support per Siena polling cited in the source. finance/ideas/idea-bloom-energy.md and finance/ideas/idea-ge-vernova.md both treat AI data-centre demand growth as structurally unconstrained — the central assumption behind both bull cases — and their Bear Case tables already list several supply-side risks (IRA/ITC policy risk for Bloom, methane/carbon regulation for GEV) but neither mentions demand-side political backlash against DC construction itself. The AI & Tech source page has already been reverse-linked into the regional power model (ai-tech/model-dc-power-regional-balance-2026-06-25.md), but neither Finance idea page reflects it at all. If other states follow New York's lead — plausible given the polling — it's a genuinely new category of risk to a thesis both ideas currently price as demand-unlimited.

Quiet day overall (Tuesday focus: AI & Tech + Technology) — reviewed the week's new AI & Tech source pages (Kimi K3, Trump AI executive order/Gold Eagle, TSMC's $100B additional U.S. investment) in depth; most of their cross-domain angles (China model-gap closing, DeepSeek pricing, CoWoS/TSMC capex) were already densely self-cross-linked by the ingest process itself, so no new investigation ideas surfaced beyond the one connection above.
